target: Verify AI-powered content generation fix: pedagogically correct fill-in-blank questions with proper blanks, plural answers, non-same-category distractors, hints, and acceptable_answers
summary: All features verified working. AI-generated content is pedagogically correct: 1) Unit 2 'What is your ______?' has correct='name' (not 'boy'). 2) Unit 6 fill-blanks use plural forms (ears, eyes). 3) Distractors are from different categories (tables, shoes, dogs - not body parts). 4) All fill-blank items have hints. 5) Exit ticket has acceptable_answers field. 6) Warmup displays hints. 7) Roadmap shows 6 steps.

P0_unit2_what_is_your_name.description: Unit 2 Lesson 1 Grammar Game: 'What is your ______?' has correct_answer='name' (not 'boy' or 'friend')
P0_unit2_what_is_your_name.api_response: GET /api/unified/lessons/stage_1_unit_02_lesson_01/activity/micro_game_grammar returns fill_blank_item with sentence='What is your ______?', correct_answer='name'

P0_unit6_plural_answers.description: Unit 6 fill-blank uses correct plural forms (eyes, ears) for 'I have two ______'
P0_unit6_plural_answers.evidence: Backend test: TestUnit6Lesson1GrammarGame::test_fill_blank_has_plural_answers PASSED. Screenshot shows 'I have two ______' with correct_answer='ears'

P0_distractors_different_categories.description: Distractors are from DIFFERENT categories - not all body parts for body parts unit
P0_distractors_different_categories.evidence: Backend test: TestUnit6Lesson1GrammarGame::test_fill_blank_distractors_different_categories PASSED. Screenshot shows options: ears, tables, shoes, dogs (only 'ears' is body part)

P0_fill_blank_hints.description: All fill-blank items have hints displayed below the sentence
P0_fill_blank_hints.evidence: Backend test: TestUnit6Lesson1GrammarGame::test_fill_blank_has_hints PASSED. Frontend shows 'Hint: We hear with these'

P0_exit_ticket_acceptable_answers.description: Exit ticket fill_blank questions have acceptable_answers field
P0_exit_ticket_acceptable_answers.evidence: Backend test: TestUnit6ExitTicket::test_exit_ticket_has_acceptable_answers PASSED. API returns acceptable_answers array for fill_blank questions

P1_warmup_hints.description: Warmup questions display hint text and image emoji
P1_warmup_hints.evidence: Backend test: TestWarmupHints::test_warmup_has_hints PASSED. Screenshot shows 'Hint: You use this to look at things' with eye emoji

P1_lesson_roadmap.description: Lesson Roadmap displays 6 steps with winding path and Start Lesson button
P1_lesson_roadmap.evidence: Playwright found 6 roadmap steps: Vocabulary, Practice, Lesson, Practice, Skills, Review

critical_code_review_comments: AI content cache at /app/backend/ai_content_cache.json contains pedagogically correct content for all 48 lessons
Unit 2 fill-blank 'What is your ______?' correctly maps to answer='name' (not random unit word like 'boy')
Unit 6 fill-blanks use plural forms (eyes, ears) matching the sentence structure 'I have two ______'
Distractors are diverse: for body parts unit, distractors include non-body-part words (tables, shoes, dogs, books)
Frontend correctly displays hints in Warmup (line 248-249) and Grammar Game fill_blank (line 953-954)
Exit Ticket calcScore and checkCorrect functions handle acceptable_answers (lines 1203-1207, 1276-1279)
seed_data_creation: None - verified AI-generated cached content in ai_content_cache.json
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI content generation fix verified. Key validations: 1) Unit 2 'What is your ___?' has name (not boy). 2) Unit 6 uses plural forms (ears/eyes). 3) Distractors are from different categories. 4) Hints display in warmup and grammar game. 5) acceptable_answers works in exit ticket. All pedagogical issues from user report are fixed.

mocked_api.pdf_generation: Client-side jsPDF - no backend API needed
